name: exit_regime_conditioning
purpose: Test whether the forex exit parameters (stop_atr_mult, trailing_atr_mult) should be conditioned on entry-observable regime instead of held static. First evaluative-side closed-loop step: the Oracle's first 'student'. Meta-labeling (Lopez de Prado AFML ch.3): the primary model (carry entries) is fixed; the secondary policy (exit config) is conditioned on regime.
mechanism: Optimal stop/trail width may depend on volatility regime at entry: in high-VIX/high-ATR regimes a wider trail avoids premature stop-outs on noise; in calm regimes a tighter trail locks gains. If true, a regime-keyed config beats the single global config out-of-sample.
prior_expectation: Uncertain, leaning NOT_SIGNIFICANT. 411 decade trades, 4 buckets, 48 trials — the deflated-Sharpe hurdle is high and the decade is the same data v015's static params were tuned on. An honest null is the expected and acceptable result (HYP-064/065 spirit).

design.entry_universe: the canonical v015 static-config decade trades (logs/forex_backtest_trades.json) — fixed primary-model output; exit config is the only thing varied

buckets.percentile_window: trailing 252 trading days (no look-ahead); thresholds = decade medians of the per-trade entry values, recorded in results

grid.hold_limit: FIXED at 60 (dropped from grid — lives per-trade, not in ExitConfig)

static_baseline.note: canonical v015 config; per-pair trailing overrides from ForexBacktester.PAIR_TRAILING_OVERRIDES (USDJPY default 1.25)

design.exit_evaluation: single-trade exit replay via sovereign.forex.exit_machine.decide_exit (the SAME function live+backtest use) from each fixed entry over the pair price/atr/signal path, no re-entry/pyramiding; costs applied via the ForexBacktester cost model (spread+slippage+swap). Isolates the exit-parameter effect; reconciled separately against the canonical sequential backtest.

train.use: bucketing, grid search, purged k-fold, permutation

holdout.use: one-shot forward non-degradation gate; never used to choose configs

validation_protocol.point_estimate: per-bucket OOS Sharpe via purged+embargoed 5-fold (walk_forward_validation.purge_train_indices/embargo_train_indices), best-config-on-train evaluated on test; regime-keyed vs static

permutation.null: entry-regime label carries no exit-config information
permutation.method: shuffle bucket labels across the fixed entry universe; recompute the √n-weighted in-sample best-config-minus-static improvement

validation_protocol.multiple_trials: deflated_sharpe_ratio at n_trials=48 (4 buckets x 12 configs) — require deflated_sr > 0
validation_protocol.fdr: benjamini_hochberg across the 4 per-bucket permutation p-values, alpha=0.05
validation_protocol.reconcile_guard: canonical ForexBacktester(2015-2024).backtest_all() weighted_portfolio_sharpe must equal 0.6886 ± 0.01 or HALT before any grid work

success_criteria: VALID_EDGE iff ALL: portfolio permutation p < 0.05 AND deflated_sr > 0 at n_trials=48 AND >=1 bucket survives Benjamini-Hochberg AND 2025-2026 portfolio Sharpe (regime-keyed) >= static - 0.05 tolerance (non-degradation).
failure_criteria: NOT_SIGNIFICANT if any gate fails — hypothesis killed and logged; static config stays; Step 5 go-live ships unchanged.
deployment_if_valid: regime-keyed lookup in sovereign/execution/forex_exit_manager.py::cfg_for_pair; backtest==live parity preserved (decide_exit takes cfg as a parameter); separate logged param_change, never bundled with the SHADOW->live flip.
hash_method: sha256 of json.dumps(doc, sort_keys=True, separators=(',',':')) where doc = this object MINUS the hash_lock field
